case_name: Shultz, A. v. Sky Zone, LLC, Aplt.

summary.brief: Shultz, A. v. Sky Zone, LLC, Aplt.. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: In February 2019, Jennifer Santiago took her two children to a Sky Zone located in Philadelphia.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: That day, three-year-old Isabella Santiago was injured when an adult entered an area designated for toddlers and jumped on Isabella’s trampoline, throwing her off the trampoline and causing a fracture of her knee.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The orders of the Superior Court are affirmed.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Parents, as natural guardians, do not have an inherent authority to 'intermeddle' with the property of their minor children, which includes legal causes of action.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: In August 2018, Ryan Shultz took his five-year-old son, Rocco, to a Sky Zone location in Philadelphia.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Rocco was injured when, contrary to Sky Zone’s rules, another patron jumped on the trampoline that Rocco was using.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Our conclusion that parents cannot bind their minor children to an agreement to arbitrate their claims obviates the need to address the other terms of the arbitration provision.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Isabella Santiago was injured when an adult entered an area designated for toddlers and jumped on her trampoline, causing her to be thrown off and sustain a knee fracture.
initial_state.initial_working_problem: Knee fracture due to trampoline accident

requestables.name: X-ray of affected area
requestables.description: To assess for fractures or dislocations

reveal.result_summary: Imaging performed but findings not detailed in judgment

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Rocco presents with injury from trampoline park

decision_points.prompt: What legal action should be taken regarding the arbitration agreement?

options.description: Challenge the enforceability of the arbitration agreement
options.clinical_reasoning: Minors cannot be bound to arbitration agreements without court approval

options.description: Accept the arbitration agreement as binding
options.clinical_reasoning: Parents do not have authority to bind minors to arbitration

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant accepted arbitration agreement as binding
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Believed it was a valid forum selection

expected_action_court.description: Challenge the enforceability of the arbitration agreement
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Minors require court protection for legal claims

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Parents cannot bind minors to arbitration without court approval
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Court should have been involved to protect minor's interests
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Minors' legal disability requires court oversight

key_findings: Parents cannot bind minors to arbitration agreements
Court oversight required for minor's legal claims

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Acceptance of arbitration agreement without court approval
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Court involvement would have protected minor's interests

allegations.allegation: Injury due to negligence at trampoline park
Parents cannot bind minors to arbitration agreements

court_findings.finding: Arbitration agreement not enforceable against minors
court_findings.legal_significance: Parents cannot bind minors to arbitration agreements

court_findings.finding: Parents lack authority to bind minors to arbitration
court_findings.legal_significance: Minors require court protection for legal claims
